task_completion.description: Verify the YDBGui web interface is open and running.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A web browser window is open displaying the YDBGui interface.

task_completion.description: Verify the agent navigated to the VA Drug Class global ^PS(50.605).
task_completion.visual_evidence: The YDBGui interface shows the global ^PS(50.605) or 'VA DRUG CLASS' data being viewed or queried.

task_completion.description: Verify the text file report is created at the correct path.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A text editor or terminal shows a file named 'drug_class_audit.txt' being edited or saved in the '/home/ga/Documents/' directory.

task_completion.description: Verify the report contains at least 5 distinct drug classifications.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The text file contains 5 or more distinct lines/entries representing different drug classifications.

task_completion.description: Verify the report includes at least one target category (CN or CV).
task_completion.visual_evidence: At least one entry in the text file has a code that starts with 'CN' (Central Nervous System) or 'CV' (Cardiovascular).

task_completion.description: Verify the entries follow the required formatting.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The entries in the text file strictly follow the format 'Code: [CODE] - Description: [DESCRIPTION]'.

integrity.description: Verify the agent used the YDBGui web interface to find the data.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots should show the agent actively browsing or querying the database within the YDBGui web interface, rather than extracting data purely via terminal MUMPS commands.

integrity.description: Verify the data in the report comes from the database and is not hallucinated.
integrity.visual_evidence: The drug codes and descriptions in the text file should match the data visible in the YDBGui screenshots.

integrity.description: Verify the agent actually created and saved the file.
integrity.visual_evidence: The agent uses a text editor (like nano, gedit, or vim) or standard file redirection to create the file, rather than just printing the expected output to the terminal screen.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The target VistA global is ^PS(50.605), which corresponds to the 'VA DRUG CLASS' FileMan file. The agent must use the YDBGui web interface to view this global. The output must be saved to '/home/ga/Documents/drug_class_audit.txt' and contain at least 5 entries. At least one entry must have a code starting with 'CN' or 'CV'. The required format for each entry is exactly 'Code: [CODE] - Description: [DESCRIPTION]'.
